title: Mapping Celestia’s Network
text:
“The Lord has been and is very kind to me in giving me so many friends may I ever be grateful for all his benefits.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - May 17, 1867

title: Celestia’s World
text:
“My throat troubles me considerable this winter. I begin to be afraid that it is going to be something serious. It hurts me to talk after talking so much in school, I have been very quiet.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - January 9, 1867

title: Mapping Celestia’s Boarding
text:
“The school room has been very uncomfortable and I was very glad when the last lesson was heard and I ready to come to my boarding place I thought certainly it would rain, sat up some time wanting for it to rain then retired feeling that the goodness of God was about my pathway.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - July 17, 1866

library(sf)

Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE

library(tidygeocoder)
library(USAboundaries)
library(tidyverse)

"Geocode locations"

[1] "Geocode locations"

locations <- geo(c("Conesus"))

Passing 1 address to the Nominatim single address geocoder
Query completed in: 1 seconds

locations

# A tibble: 1 × 3
  address   lat  long
  <chr>   <dbl> <dbl>
1 Conesus  42.7 -77.7

"Convert locations to sf object"

[1] "Convert locations to sf object"

locations_sf <- st_as_sf(locations, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)
locations_sf

Simple feature collection with 1 feature and 1 field
Geometry type: POINT
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: -77.67639 ymin: 42.71895 xmax: -77.67639 ymax: 42.71895
Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
# A tibble: 1 × 2
  address             geometry
* <chr>            <POINT [°]>
1 Conesus (-77.67639 42.71895)

"Get a Base Map"

[1] "Get a Base Map"

newyork <- us_counties(states = "New York")

"Reproject the data"

[1] "Reproject the data"

# Find EPSG code for New York
state_plane(state = "NY")

[1] 32116

# Project
newyork_proj <- st_transform(newyork, "EPSG:32116")

# Map the data
ggplot() + 
  geom_sf(data = newyork_proj) + 
  labs(title = "Map of New York counties",
       subtitle = "Conesus, Livingston County, New York") + 
  theme_minimal()

title: The Webster Family
text:
“How wise and good in God to grant us friends, he has bound us together by the ties of blood that can never be sundered We should be ever trying to do each other good to help each other on in our journey to the kingdom”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - March 31, 1866

Sickness in the Family
“My heart is sad at the remembrance, that for the third time our family circle was broken four months ago today, then the head of our family our noble Christian father was torn from our embrace by the rude hand of Death My life seems to be a blank since then. I am so disconsolate and do not know what to set myself about.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - April 1, 1866
“Since my last birthday sad, bitter change have taken place A loved father has been taken from us, We left a fatherless group of children with our widowed mother Oh how deeply we feel his loss. Since then sickness has been in our midst of we fear? that our family will perhaps be broken again…A short time before twelve some one rapped at the door, I went to the doors and was surprised to see Nelson there, he brought the news that both Lib and Wet were sick, he said Wet was very bad off; her lungs are so bad, he said that she had a very bad day a couple of days ago.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - July 30-31, 1866
“Early this forenoon it rained some and has looked as though it was preparing for a heavy rain. Carrie and I sat up with Wet last night. Sue was up the most of the time too I sat on the edge of the bed fanning her and holding her hand occasionally rubbing her stomach when I was not waiting on her as she was vomiting from ten o’clock till four. She says to me I want you to stay with me as much as you can. She thinks we will not have her with us long. In the midst of her severe pain she exclaimed several times “bless the Lord” that it was no worse.”
Celestia Webster’s Diary -October 10, 1866

title: Mapping Trips to Aunt Zerviah’s
text:
“They have all with the exception of Mother Lib and me gone to Aunt Zerviah to spend the evening I tired out every day by dark as I have no place to lie down and rest during the day. Lib and I am going to retire early”
Celestia Webster’s Diary - November 13, 1867

title: Mapping Trips to Aunt Zerviah’s
text:
Trips to Aunt Zerviah
